name: github-actions
description: Security-hardened GitHub Actions workflow patterns and CI/CD best practices

decisions.decision: One workflow per concern with deny-all permissions and concurrency control
decisions.rationale: Separation of concerns keeps workflows maintainable and auditable. Deny-all permissions prevents accidental privilege escalation. Concurrency groups avoid wasted CI minutes on stale runs.
decisions.details:
Organise as: ci.yml (PR checks), release.yml (tag-triggered), optionally codeql.yml, docs.yml.

Set permissions: {} at the workflow level to deny everything, then grant per-job with minimum required permissions.

Use concurrency groups on PR workflows to cancel stale runs:

concurrency:
  group: ci-${{ github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: true

Add workflow_dispatch on release workflows for manual re-runs.

Set cancel-in-progress: false on deployment workflows to avoid partial deploys.

decisions.decision: Pin all third-party actions to full commit SHA with version comment
decisions.rationale: Mutable tags (@v4, @main, @latest) can be moved by action maintainers — a compromised action tag is a supply chain attack. Full SHA is immutable. The version comment provides human readability.
decisions.details:
Format:
  uses: actions/checkout@11bd71901bbe5b1630ceea73d27597364c9af683 # v4.2.2

Never use:
  uses: actions/checkout@main
  uses: actions/checkout@v4

Enable Dependabot for the github-actions ecosystem to get automated SHA-bump PRs:

# .github/dependabot.yml
version: 2
updates:
  - package-ecosystem: github-actions
    directory: /
    schedule:
      interval: weekly

This keeps actions current without manual SHA tracking.

decisions.decision: Deny all at workflow level; grant minimum per-job; use OIDC over long-lived secrets
decisions.rationale: Default GITHUB_TOKEN permissions are overly broad. Explicit per-job declarations make privilege requirements auditable. OIDC eliminates long-lived credential storage entirely.
decisions.details:
Workflow level:
  permissions: {}

CI jobs:
  permissions:
    contents: read

Release jobs:
  permissions:
    contents: write
    id-token: write    # OIDC for Sigstore/cloud auth
    packages: write    # only if pushing to GHCR

For cloud provider auth (AWS, GCP, Azure), use OIDC with id-token: write instead of storing access keys as secrets. This eliminates credential rotation and reduces blast radius of a token leak.

decisions.decision: Prevent injection attacks, restrict untrusted code execution, and limit token scope
decisions.rationale: GitHub Actions workflows are a common attack surface. Event data in run: blocks enables shell injection. pull_request_target grants write access to fork PRs. Defence in depth requires multiple controls.
decisions.details:
Rules:
- Never use ${{ github.event.*.body }} or similar event data directly in run: blocks — pass via env: to prevent shell injection.
- Never use pull_request_target with untrusted code checkout — it grants write access to fork PRs.
- Restrict GITHUB_TOKEN scope: contents: read for CI, contents: write only for release.
- Use environment protection rules for production deployments.
- Enable required reviewers on environments with secrets.
- Audit third-party actions before adoption — prefer actions from verified creators or with significant community usage.

decisions.decision: Semantic versioning with Conventional Commits for automated version bumps
decisions.rationale: Semver communicates compatibility guarantees. Conventional Commits make version bumps deterministic and auditable — the commit history IS the changelog source.
decisions.details:
Tag format: vMAJOR.MINOR.PATCH (e.g., v1.2.3). GoReleaser and most release tools enforce this.

Version bump mapping from Conventional Commits:
  fix:  → PATCH
  feat: → MINOR
  feat!: or BREAKING CHANGE: → MAJOR
  chore:, docs:, test: → no bump

Pre-release tags: v1.0.0-rc1, v1.0.0-beta.2 — detected automatically by release tooling.

For manual tagging, always tag from main after PR merge. For automated tagging, use go-semantic-release or similar action triggered on push to main.

decisions.decision: Continuous snapshot releases from main with dev version metadata
decisions.rationale: Snapshot releases give users and CI consumers access to the latest changes without waiting for a tagged release. Deterministic version format makes snapshots sortable and traceable.
decisions.details:
Snapshot version format: {major}.{minor}.{next_patch}-dev.{commits_since_tag}+{short_hash}
Example: 0.0.59-dev.7+abc1234

Implementation:
- Trigger on push to main (not tags).
- Skip if the commit already has a release tag.
- Delete previous snapshot release/tag before creating new one (single rolling snapshot).
- Mark as prerelease so it does not become 'latest' on GitHub.
- Include same artifacts as full release for testing parity.

decisions.decision: Enforce PR workflow, required status checks, and up-to-date branches on main
decisions.rationale: Branch protection prevents accidental direct pushes, ensures CI passes before merge, and catches integration issues from stale branches.
decisions.details:
Configure on main:
- Require pull request before merging (no direct pushes).
- Require status checks to pass: lint, test, vuln (match your CI job names).
- Require branches to be up to date before merging.
- Dismiss stale pull request approvals when new commits are pushed.
- Restrict who can push to matching branches (maintainers and release bots only).
- Optionally require signed commits for supply chain integrity.
